title: L9 File Signals (per-file evidence record)
description: The signals each materialized (extracted/existing) file generates for the dev pack. Emitted by scripts/extract_files.py as .ai/file-signals.yaml alongside the target filetree. A signal records what a present file PROVES — which contract it satisfies, which invariants it enforces, which commands validate it, which DPK-1.0 layer it populates, and its assembly state — so downstream agents route work on evidence, not guesses. Presence is not conformance: a `made` state means the file was materialized, NOT that it is verified; `needs_review` flags corruption (smart quotes, `...` elision) that a human/agent must resolve before the file is trusted.

path.description: Repo-relative target path of the materialized file.

state.description: Assembly state. made = content materialized into target/. made_needs_review = materialized but corruption detected (do not trust until resolved). missing_source = declared extract/existing but no source block found (reported, never fabricated). deferred = spec-deferred. remaining = build/adapt, map-only, not written.

satisfies_contract.description: The contract_ref from file_plan this file implements (maps to a contracts[].id).

enforces_invariants.description: Invariant ids whose enforcement names this file/command (from spec.invariants[].enforcement).

validated_by.description: Command names (spec.commands[].name) whose cmd references this file — the evidence that proves it.

populates_layer.description: The DPK-1.0 layer this file populates.

source_doc.description: Provenance: the source doc (or `repo`) the content was materialized from.

needs_review.description: Human-readable corruption finding when state = made_needs_review (e.g. smart-quotes present). The extractor never silently rewrites code; this is a report for a human/agent to resolve.
